questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Which of the following diagrams represents the correct relationship between Books, Novels and Dictionaries?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Which of the following combinations of circles best represents Athletes, Sprinters and Marathon runners? [NIFT (PG) 2013]

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Find the region that represents those rural students who are not intelligent? [UP B.Ed. 2010]

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In the following diagram, there are three circles intersecting one another. Each intersecting region represents a class of people. Each intersecting region has been denoted by a digit. Study the diagram carefully and answer the given question. [SSC (Multitasking) 2008] Find out those Engineers who are neither Indian nor Teacher.

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In the given diagram, circle represents professionals, square represents dancers, triangle represents musicians and rectangle represents Europeans. Different regions in the diagram are numbered 1 to 11. Who among the following is neither a dancer nor a musician but is professional and not a European? [SSC (CGL) 2013]

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In the diagram given below, N represents non-degree holders, D represents degree holders and C represents the computer programmers. Among these, which number indicates the programmers, who are neither degree holders nor non-degree holders?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: How many educated people are employed? [SSC (10+2) 2012]

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In the above diagram, circle P represents hardworking people, circle Q represents intelligent people, circle R represents truthful people and circle S represents honest people. Which region represents the people who are intelligent, honest and truthful but not hardworking? [UPSC (CSAT) 2012]
